understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, then proceed through all levels until the game reports WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN) shift only the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) per move in the chosen direction, subject to collisions with walls/boundaries and interactions with adjacent objects. Some adjacent objects can be pushed if directly in the movement direction, causing both to shift together if space allows; walls/boundaries can block movement in non-uniform ways. With multiple selectables, control is switched using CLICK at the exact pixel-coordinate position of the target selectable; the clicked piece becomes the active 'player' piece for subsequent moves. The movement_blocked flags were only hints about adjacency and did not guarantee success or failure of a move.
